acceptance_criteria.given: a dev-report with baseline_head_sha set, dev.files_modified=["agents/qa.md"], dev.files_created=[], and baseline_dirty_snapshot=""
acceptance_criteria.when: git diff --name-only <baseline_head_sha> returns agents/qa.md, commands/dev.md, agents/dev.md
acceptance_criteria.then: agents/qa.md Step 5 provenance check raises files_modified_underreport_violation for commands/dev.md and agents/dev.md (both in diff, both absent from dev.files_modified ∪ dev.files_created, not in baseline_dirty_paths); severity: critical; agents/qa.md does NOT raise the violation for itself (listed in files_modified)

check.expect: prose describing reverse-direction check that iterates diff_files against dev.files_modified ∪ dev.files_created and raises files_modified_underreport_violation with severity critical for any absent path not in baseline_dirty_paths

acceptance_criteria.given: two per-worker dev-reports: worker-A has baseline_head_sha="abc123", worker-B has baseline_head_sha="abc123", both have matching baseline_dirty_snapshot, and each has a non-empty dev.observed_preexisting
acceptance_criteria.when: the orchestrator writes the aggregate per commands/dev.md Step 9
acceptance_criteria.then: the aggregate JSON contains top-level baseline_head_sha="abc123", top-level baseline_dirty_snapshot matching the dispatch value, and dev.observed_preexisting as a UNION of both workers' lists; AND if worker-B instead has baseline_head_sha="xyz999", the aggregate's dev.status is "blocked" with a blocking_issue citing the baseline mismatch

check.expect: baseline_head_sha, baseline_dirty_snapshot, and dev.observed_preexisting present in both the aggregate construction rule text (lines ~720-734) and the example aggregate JSON (lines ~739-756); baseline mismatch described as blocking (not warning)

acceptance_criteria.given: a dev execution where one new file is staged (git add new-file.py) and a second new file is untracked (not staged)
acceptance_criteria.when: dev derives dev.files_created per agents/dev.md derivation rules
acceptance_criteria.then: dev.files_created includes both new-file.py (from git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=A) and the untracked file (from git ls-files --others --exclude-standard); paths in baseline_dirty_snapshot parsed path list are excluded from both sources before union

check.expect: agents/dev.md line ~495 derivation rule references both git ls-files --others --exclude-standard AND git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=A as the combined files_created derivation

acceptance_criteria.given: a dev-report with dev.files_created=["staged-new.py", "untracked-new.py"] where staged-new.py was staged (in git index, not in git ls-files --others) and untracked-new.py is untracked
acceptance_criteria.when: QA runs the files_created provenance check at agents/qa.md Step 5
acceptance_criteria.then: QA checks against the combined set (git ls-files --others --exclude-standard UNION git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=A) and does NOT raise files_created_provenance_violation for staged-new.py

check.expect: agents/qa.md line ~427 QA files_created check references git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=A as part of the combined derivation set it compares against

acceptance_criteria.given: a dev-report with baseline_dirty_snapshot containing " M pre-dirty.md" (a file dirty at baseline time), dev.files_modified=[], dev.files_created=[], and dev.observed_preexisting=["pre-dirty.md"]
acceptance_criteria.then: QA does NOT raise files_modified_underreport_violation for pre-dirty.md (it is in baseline_dirty_paths); AND if a different file other.md appears in diff and is absent from dev.files_modified ∪ dev.files_created and absent from baseline_dirty_paths but present in dev.observed_preexisting, QA DOES raise files_modified_underreport_violation for other.md (observed_preexisting is not an exclusion)

check.expect: reverse check exclusion set is baseline_dirty_paths (parsed from baseline_dirty_snapshot porcelain); observed_preexisting is not listed as an exclusion in the reverse check logic

acceptance_criteria.given: a dev-report with baseline_head_sha set, dev.files_modified=[], dev.files_created=["staged-new.py"], and baseline_dirty_snapshot=""
acceptance_criteria.then: QA does NOT raise files_modified_underreport_violation for staged-new.py (it is present in dev.files_created, satisfying the dev.files_modified ∪ dev.files_created union check); no violation is fired for a diff path that appears in dev.files_created even if absent from dev.files_modified

check.expect: reverse check compares diff_files against dev.files_modified ∪ dev.files_created (union), not against dev.files_modified alone; any path in dev.files_created satisfies the union check and does not raise the violation
